 {"id":"bd-3t3ct","title":"[PERF-5] Adaptive FPS under CPU pressure via /proc/loadavg monitoring","description":"## Problem

On an overloaded machine (many agents running, heavy compilation, etc.), the TUI should not waste CPU cycles rendering at 60fps when the user probably cannot even see the updates.

## Solution: CPU Pressure Detection + FPS Adaptation

### Detection
- Sample /proc/loadavg every 2 seconds (Linux) or libc::getloadavg() (macOS/BSD)
- Compare 1-minute load average against available CPU count (from std::thread::available_parallelism())
- Pressure levels:
  - load < 1.0 × CPUs → Normal
  - load 1.0-2.0 × CPUs → Moderate pressure
  - load > 2.0 × CPUs → High pressure
  - load > 4.0 × CPUs → Critical pressure

### Adaptation
- Normal: 60fps (default)
- Moderate: 45fps
- High: 30fps
- Critical: 15fps

### Implementation

```rust
struct CpuPressureMonitor {
    cpu_count: usize,
    last_sample: Instant,
    sample_interval: Duration,  // 2 seconds
    current_load: f64,
    pressure_level: PressureLevel,
}

impl CpuPressureMonitor {
    fn sample(&mut self) -> PressureLevel {
        if self.last_sample.elapsed() < self.sample_interval {
            return self.pressure_level;
        }
        self.last_sample = Instant::now();
        
        #[cfg(target_os = "linux")]
        {
            // Read /proc/loadavg: "0.52 0.64 0.71 1/234 12345"
            // Parse first field (1-minute load average)
            if let Ok(content) = std::fs::read_to_string("/proc/loadavg") {
                if let Some(load_str) = content.split_whitespace().next() {
                    if let Ok(load) = load_str.parse::<f64>() {
                        self.current_load = load;
                    }
                }
            }
        }
        
        #[cfg(any(target_os = "macos", target_os = "freebsd"))]
        {
            // libc::getloadavg() — standard POSIX, available on macOS and BSD
            let mut loadavg = [0.0f64; 1];
            unsafe {
                if libc::getloadavg(loadavg.as_mut_ptr(), 1) == 1 {
                    self.current_load = loadavg[0];
                }
            }
        }
        
        let ratio = self.current_load / self.cpu_count as f64;
        self.pressure_level = match ratio {
            r if r < 1.0 => PressureLevel::Normal,
            r if r < 2.0 => PressureLevel::Moderate,
            r if r < 4.0 => PressureLevel::High,
            _ => PressureLevel::Critical,
        };
        self.pressure_level
    }
}
```

### Integration with Frame Budget (PERF-4)
- CPU pressure provides a FLOOR for the frame budget fidelity level
- If CPU pressure is Critical, fidelity cannot be higher than Degraded
- Frame budget can further reduce fidelity if individual frames exceed budget
- Combined: min(cpu_pressure_level, frame_budget_level)

### Platform Support
- Linux: /proc/loadavg (always available, zero-allocation read)
- macOS/BSD: libc::getloadavg() (POSIX standard, no special crate needed)
- Windows/other: disable (always Normal)
- Use #[cfg(target_os)] for compile-time platform selection

### Why This Can Depend on PERF-3 Instead of PERF-4
- PERF-5 only needs the FrameTimingStats counter (for sample interval gating) and the PressureLevel enum
- It does NOT need the full FrameBudget state machine to be implemented
- The integration point is: FrameBudget::effective_fidelity() = min(budget_fidelity, cpu_pressure_level)
- This min() can be added to PERF-4 as a simple method, and PERF-5 provides the cpu_pressure_level input
- **However**: keeping the PERF-4 dependency is cleaner because PERF-4 defines the RenderFidelity enum and the effective_fidelity() method that PERF-5 feeds into

## Files to Modify
- src/interactive.rs: CpuPressureMonitor struct, integration with FrameBudget

## Dependencies
- Depends on PERF-4 (frame budget — this layer feeds into budget decisions with effective_fidelity())

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] CpuPressureMonitor reads /proc/loadavg on Linux
- [ ] CpuPressureMonitor uses libc::getloadavg() on macOS/BSD (not sysctl)
- [ ] Pressure levels correctly mapped from load/cpu_count ratio
- [ ] FPS adapted based on pressure level
- [ ] Integration with frame budget state machine via effective_fidelity()
- [ ] Unit tests for pressure level calculation (injectable load value, not reading /proc in tests)
- [ ] Unit tests for combined pressure + budget decision
- [ ] Graceful no-op on non-Linux/macOS platforms (always Normal)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:14:31.312915658Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T04:26:19.432381266Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T04:26:19.432346571Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":1032,"issue_id":"bd-3t3ct","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Absorbed into PERF-4 (bd-anewk). CPU pressure detection is now a simple additional signal within the frame budget state machine, not a separate subsystem. Closing as merged.","created_at":"2026-02-13T04:26:16Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-3tb30","title":"[SEC-3.4] Enforcement state machine: allow/harden/prompt/deny/terminate with hysteresis","description":"## Background
A score is only useful if mapped to deterministic enforcement actions with anti-flapping safeguards.

## Scope
- Define score bands to actions (`allow`, `harden`, `prompt`, `deny`, `terminate`).
- Implement cooldown/hysteresis to prevent oscillation under borderline behavior.
- Integrate with capability policy precedence and fail-closed semantics.

## Deliverables
- Enforcement state machine implementation + transition tests.
- Action mapping table per policy profile.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] State transitions are deterministic and reproducible.
- [ ] Borderline score jitter does not cause action flapping.
- [ ] Policy precedence with manual overrides is documented and tested.","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description is implemented fully with no feature loss
[ ] Unit tests added/updated for success, failure, edge cases, and determinism where applicable
[ ] E2E scripts added/updated for benign flow, adversarial flow, and rollback/recovery flow relevant to this bead
[ ] E2E runs emit structured JSONL logs with: timestamp, issue_id, extension_id, capability, policy_profile, score, reason_codes, action, latency_ms, correlation_id, and redaction_summary
[ ] Artifact manifest is deterministic and linked in bead comments (paths + checksums/identifiers)
[ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test (plus targeted conformance/security suites)
[ ] If runtime behavior changed, docs/config examples are updated; if no behavior changed, explicitly record N/A rationale for e2e changes","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-14T04:39:40.722582060Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-14T10:43:44.832130855Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["enforcement","runtime-detection","security"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3tb30","depends_on_id":"bd-2vbax","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-14T04:39:58.403494951Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3tb30","depends_on_id":"bd-3f1ab","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-14T04:39:58.176903869Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":1418,"issue_id":"bd-3tb30","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"TopazFalcon claiming bd-3tb30 (SEC-3.4). Will implement: (1) enforcement state machine with 5 states (Allow/Harden/Prompt/Deny/Terminate), (2) score-band-to-action mapping table per policy profile, (3) cooldown/hysteresis to prevent flapping under borderline scores, (4) integration with capability policy precedence and fail-closed semantics.","created_at":"2026-02-14T10:30:18Z"},{"id":1420,"issue_id":"bd-3tb30","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Verification note from RainyMill: All 3 acceptance criteria appear met. 27 enforcement tests pass (cargo test --lib enforcement): deterministic sequences, no-flap under jitter, cooldown, de-escalation, terminate-is-terminal, policy merge precedence. EnforcementStateMachine with hysteresis (de_escalation_margin=0.10, cooldown_calls=3), score bands per profile (safe/balanced/permissive), and merge_with_policy() all implemented and tested.","created_at":"2026-02-14T10:43:44Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3tu","title":"Crates publishing workflow for pi_agent_rust + libs","description":"Add crates.io publish workflows/metadata for pi_agent_rust, asupersync, rich_rust, charmed_rust; plan migration from path deps to versioned crates.","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
[ ] Unit tests cover core success/failure + edge cases for this bead (add regression coverage if applicable)
[ ] Integration/E2E script exercises the end-to-end path (or explicitly marks N/A with rationale in notes) and emits detailed JSONL logs + artifacts per bd-4u9
[ ] Logs include inputs, outputs, timing, and redaction summary; artifacts list is deterministic
[ ] Any new fixtures/golden outputs are deterministic and documented
[ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test
[ ] Docs/fixtures updated if behavior or UX changes","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T02:18:50.598149638Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T19:28:33.293934169Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T19:49:42.660939587Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":157,"issue_id":"bd-3tu","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Rust-crates-publishing checklist (use for pi_agent_rust + asupersync + rich_rust + charmed_rust):
1) Ensure Cargo.toml metadata: license, readme, repository, keywords/categories, publish=true.
2) Tag/version alignment: tag vX.Y.Z must match package.version.
3) CI: publish job gated on tags; requires CARGO_REGISTRY_TOKEN; skip prerelease tags.
4) Order: publish dependency crates first (asupersync/rich_rust/charmed_rust), then pi_agent_rust.
5) Validate with cargo package + cargo publish --dry-run.","created_at":"2026-02-03T17:02:29Z"}]}
